evals.prompt: I need to build a user authentication system for my Node.js app. It should support email/password login, JWT tokens, and a password reset flow. The login and reset flows touch different files so they can be built at the same time. Use the team workflow skill to set this up.
evals.expected_output: Claude confirms with user via AskUserQuestion showing dev workflow, 4 phases, 2 parallel developers, 2 reviewers, 1 spawned leader. File-path handoff and round limits in worker prompts.
assertions: Output includes a proposal/question to the user (AskUserQuestion) BEFORE describing team creation steps
Leader is listed as a spawned agent member, not described as 'you' or 'the current session'
Both reviewer-a and reviewer-b appear in the team design
Two developers (developer-1/developer-2) are proposed for login and reset flows respectively
At least one worker prompt instructs sending file paths only, not full content, in handoff messages
At least one reviewer or developer prompt mentions a maximum of 3 rounds or tracks round number

evals.prompt: Can you research the best approach for implementing a distributed rate limiter? I want a proper research workflow with peer review — two reviewers who can push back on each other.
evals.expected_output: Claude confirms research workflow (research→review), 1 researcher, 2 reviewers who can disagree, 1 spawned leader. Reviewer prompts explicitly allow disagreement and prohibit file writing.
assertions: Output identifies this as a research workflow (not development), with phases: research → review
Output includes a proposal/question to the user before describing team creation
Reviewer prompts explicitly state they can disagree with the other reviewer and should not defer
Reviewer prompts instruct reviewers NOT to write code or files — feedback text only
Leader is listed as a spawned agent member, not described as 'you' or 'the current session'

evals.prompt: Use team workflow to implement two independent features for my Python Flask app: (1) a CSV export endpoint that writes to export_handler.py, and (2) a webhook notification system that writes to webhook_handler.py. These are completely separate files.
evals.expected_output: Claude proposes 2 parallel developers with exclusive file ownership. Each developer prompt names only its own file and prohibits touching the other's. Leader is a spawned agent. File-path handoffs in prompts.
assertions: Two separate developers (developer-1 and developer-2, or equivalent) appear in the team design
Each developer prompt specifies exclusive file ownership and instructs NOT to touch the other developer's files
At least one worker prompt instructs sending file paths only, not full content, in handoff messages
Leader is listed as a spawned agent member, not described as 'you' or 'the current session'
Output includes a proposal/question to the user before describing team creation
